sections.paragraph: f. (root דָּבַכ No. 2) slander, calumny. הֹוצִיא דִבָּה to spread slander, Num 14:36 Pro 10:18. The genitive which follows has either an active sense [i.e. of the slanderer], e. g. Psa 31:14, בִּי שָׁמַעְתִּי דִּבַּת רַבִּים “for I heard the slander of many,” Jer 20:10 or a passive [of the person slandered], Num 13:32 14:37 Gen 37:2 Pro 25:10, וְרִבָּֽתְךָ לֹא תָשׁוּב “(so) that thine infamy turn not away from thee.” (Arab. دَبُوبُ a secret slanderer, one who spreads calumnies. Syr. ܛܒܶܐܳ report, rumour, and ܐܰܛܒܶ to spread a rumour. Ch. טִבָּה reproach).
